name: compliance-gate-demo
description:
Self-contained, no-validator demo of the compliance gate. Five synthetic participants (one each of VERIFIED / PENDING / SUSPENDED / BLOCKED, plus a second VERIFIED counterparty) are evaluated against `AccountRecord::check_transfer_allowed` for every sender-receiver pair. The output is a 5×5 matrix of allow / reject decisions plus a named reason for each reject (`sender SUSPENDED`, `receiver BLOCKED`, …). Useful for asking: which statuses actually clear the gate? What error does an operator see for a specific reject? How does the gate's behavior change if a verified counterparty is suspended mid-flow?

Runs in milliseconds against pure-Rust `ssr-types` primitives — no Solana validator, no LiteSVM, no deployed program required.
headline: Five participants × 5 = 25 pairs; only the two VERIFIED ↔ VERIFIED transfers clear the gate, each reject carries a named reason.

steps.explanation: Run the in-process compliance gate matrix demo. Builds a synthetic participant population and exhaustively evaluates every (sender, receiver) pair.
